intro: Zygia ampla, also known as the Mexican bird's egg tree, is a species of flowering tree in the legume family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The tree has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Zygia ampla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Zygia ampla is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal disorders.
